note: In a distributed system facing a network partition, you cannot guarantee both consistency and availability at the same time.
keyFacts: Consistency and availability trade off during a partition
commonConfusions: You can always have all three
CAP is about normal operation without partitions
recalledText: When the network is partitioned, a distributed system has to choose between consistency and availability.

commonConfusions: You can always have all three

note: QoS 0 is at-most-once delivery: the sender transmits once with no acknowledgment and no retry.

note: An operation is idempotent if repeating it produces the same resulting state as doing it once.
keyFacts: Repeated execution does not change final state after the first application
recalledText: An idempotent operation reaches the same final state no matter how many times you repeat it.

note: An operation is idempotent if repeating it produces the same resulting state as doing it once.
keyFacts: Repeated execution does not change final state after the first application
recalledText: It means the same request can be repeated safely.

note: An operation is idempotent if repeating it produces the same resulting state as doing it once.
keyFacts: Repeated execution does not change final state after the first application
recalledText: I think it means an API request is cached.

note: An operation is idempotent if repeating it produces the same resulting state as doing it once.
keyFacts: Repeated execution does not change final state after the first application
recalledText: It means the server returns the same response body every time.

note: An operation is idempotent if repeating it produces the same resulting state as doing it once.
keyFacts: Repeated execution does not change final state after the first application
recalledText: Repeating it does not cause extra changes, but I did not explain what stays the same.

note: A Bloom filter is a space-efficient probabilistic structure for set membership that allows false positives but not false negatives.
recalledText: A Bloom filter is a compact probabilistic set-membership structure with false positives but no false negatives.

note: A Bloom filter is a space-efficient probabilistic structure for set membership that allows false positives but not false negatives.
recalledText: It is a compact data structure for checking whether something is in a set.

note: A Bloom filter is a space-efficient probabilistic structure for set membership that allows false positives but not false negatives.

note: A Bloom filter is a space-efficient probabilistic structure for set membership that allows false positives but not false negatives.
recalledText: A Bloom filter can miss items that are actually present, but it never says yes by mistake.

note: A Bloom filter is a space-efficient probabilistic structure for set membership that allows false positives but not false negatives.
recalledText: It probably tells you whether something is in a set, but I left out the error trade-off.

note: Osmosis is the passive movement of water across a semipermeable membrane from lower solute concentration to higher solute concentration.

note: Osmosis is the passive movement of water across a semipermeable membrane from lower solute concentration to higher solute concentration.

note: The availability heuristic is judging probability or frequency by how easily examples come to mind.

note: The Dunning-Kruger effect is the tendency for low-competence individuals to overestimate their ability because they lack the insight to recognize their deficits.

note: The Dunning-Kruger effect is the tendency for low-competence individuals to overestimate their ability because they lack the insight to recognize their deficits.

commonConfusions: Any change in the brain counts
recalledText: Hebbian plasticity is the idea that synapses strengthen when neurons activate together.

note: In Spanish, ser is generally used for identity, inherent characteristics, and origin, while estar is used for states, locations, and temporary conditions.
